news.title:
Solar Defence began work on robotics, UAV facility in Nagpur SEZ.
Facility focuses on advanced defence tech, boosting indigenous capabilities.
news.summary:

news.title:
Solar Industries India Ltd surged 3.9%, closing at ₹14,530.70 on 04 Mar.
The rise reflects robust financials and sustained growth, boosting investor confidence.
news.summary:
Solar Industries India Ltd surged 3.9%, closing at ₹14,530.70 on 04 Mar.

news.title:
Defence stocks show divergence; PSUs like HAL (-9% YTD) and BDL (-16% YTD) struggle, while private players like Data Patterns (+18% YTD) and Solar Industries (+10% YTD) gain.
Nifty Defence Index gains 4.8% YTD. Analysts highlight elevated valuations, execution challenges, and policy gaps as key factors affecting PSU stocks, favoring private players.
news.summary:
Defence stocks show divergence; PSUs like HAL (-9% YTD) and BDL (-16% YTD) struggle, while private players like Data Patterns (+18% YTD) and Solar Industries (+10% YTD) gain.
Nifty Defence Index gains 4.8% YTD. Analysts highlight elevated valuations, execution challenges, and policy gaps as key factors affecting PSU stocks, favoring private players.

news.title:
India's defence firms like Balu Forge and Solar Industries are leveraging the global 155 mm shell shortage, scaling production and exports.
India's cost-competitive shells, priced $300-$400 vs. $3,000 Western equivalents, position it as a key supplier for NATO-aligned and Asian markets.
news.summary:
India's defence firms like Balu Forge and Solar Industries are leveraging the global 155 mm shell shortage, scaling production and exports.
India's cost-competitive shells, priced $300-$400 vs. $3,000 Western equivalents, position it as a key supplier for NATO-aligned and Asian markets.

news.title:
India's missile boom highlights the crucial role of defence chemicals like propellants and explosives, now mostly produced domestically.
Key players like Solar Industries and Premier Explosives lead in manufacturing missile propellants, explosives, and defence materials, driving long-term growth.
news.summary:
India's missile boom highlights the crucial role of defence chemicals like propellants and explosives, now mostly produced domestically.
Key players like Solar Industries and Premier Explosives lead in manufacturing missile propellants, explosives, and defence materials, driving long-term growth.

news.title:
Solar Industries expects 15% annual growth, with over 20% growth in 3-5 years driven by mining and defense.
Management anticipates 6-7% demand rise in mining products, leading to 10-12% volume and 15% business growth in India.
news.summary:
Solar Industries expects 15% annual growth, with over 20% growth in 3-5 years driven by mining and defense.
Management anticipates 6-7% demand rise in mining products, leading to 10-12% volume and 15% business growth in India.

news.title:
Solar Industries hosted an earnings call on Feb 4, 2026, discussing unaudited Q3 and 9-month financial results.
The audio recording of the call is available on the company’s website for reference.

Management shared updates during a recent concall, confirming Q4 expectations.

news.title:
Goldman Sachs maintains a Buy rating on Solar Industries.
The target price for Solar Industries is set at Rs 18,360.

news.title:
Solar Industries' Q3 EBITDA rose to ₹7.1B from ₹5.3B YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 27.8% from 26.7% YoY.
news.summary:
Solar Industries' Q3 EBITDA rose to ₹7.1B from ₹5.3B YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 27.8% from 26.7% YoY.

news.title:
ICRA reaffirmed and assigned an A1 rating to Solar Industries India's Rs. 500 Cr. commercial paper.
The rating action was disclosed under SEBI Regulation 30 by the company secretary, Khushboo Pasari.
news.summary:
ICRA reaffirmed and assigned an A1 rating to Solar Industries India's Rs. 500 Cr. commercial paper.
The rating action was disclosed under SEBI Regulation 30 by the company secretary, Khushboo Pasari.

The deal enhances Solar Industries' revenue potential significantly.

The order, to be delivered in 4 years, is from international clients and unrelated parties.

news.title:
A1 Limited surged 3063% in 5 years, gaining 387% YTD in 2025. It closed at ₹1864.05, up 5% on Friday.
A1 signed a tripartite deal to supply 10,000 MT nitric acid between GNFC and Solar Industries, boosting its market role.

news.title:
Indian firms secured Rs 5,000 crore orders, showcasing leadership in defence, clean energy, and infrastructure sectors.
Key winners include L&T, HAL, HG Infra, KEC International, and Solar Industries, with varied contracts improving revenue visibility.
